nodes.title: 10x Factor
nodes.blurb: The 10x Factor is the value-differential threshold Pereira identifies as the entry ticket to exponential growth.

nodes.title: AARRR Framework
nodes.blurb: AARRR is a framework for measuring and optimizing the customer lifecycle in digital businesses.

nodes.title: Affiliate Marketing
nodes.blurb: Affiliate marketing is a business model where third-party publishers promote another company's products and earn a commission on resulting sales or actions.

nodes.title: Aggregator Business Model
nodes.blurb: An aggregator business model creates value by collecting information, goods, or services from many providers and presenting them in one place for end customers

nodes.title: Anchor Pricing
nodes.blurb: Anchor pricing displays a higher reference price alongside the actual selling price to create a favorable comparison and make the selling price feel like a bargain.

nodes.title: Anchoring Bias
nodes.blurb: Anchoring bias is the tendency to rely too heavily on the first piece of information received when making a decision, even if that information is irrelevant or arbitrary.

nodes.title: Annual Contract Value
nodes.blurb: Annual Contract Value (ACV) is the average yearly revenue received from each customer contract, excluding one-time fees.

nodes.title: API Business Model
nodes.blurb: An API (Application Programming Interface) business model is the strategic framework through which a company monetizes its APIs or creates value through API-related activities.

nodes.title: API Economy
nodes.blurb: The API economy encompasses the business models, strategies, and ecosystems built around the development, distribution, and consumption of Application Programming Interfaces (APIs).

nodes.title: Automation-Based Business Model
nodes.blurb: An automation-based business model relies on algorithms or automated systems to perform the operations that would otherwise require continuous human labor.

nodes.title: Availability Heuristic
nodes.blurb: The availability heuristic is the mental shortcut of judging the likelihood or frequency of an event by how easily examples come to mind.

nodes.blurb: TAM/SAM/SOM is a three-layer market sizing tactic used to demonstrate that a market is large enough to support the financial investment required to pursue it.

nodes.title: Transaction-Based API Model
nodes.blurb: The transaction-based API model charges developers based on the number of transactions or discrete interactions made through the API.

nodes.title: Triple Bottom Line
nodes.blurb: The Triple Bottom Line expands the definition of value creation beyond financial performance to include social impact (people) and environmental impact (planet) alongside profit.

nodes.title: Unique Selling Proposition
nodes.blurb: A Unique Selling Proposition (USP) is a concise, strategic statement that captures why a prospective customer should choose this product over alternatives.

nodes.title: Viral Coefficient
nodes.blurb: The Viral Coefficient (often called the K-Factor) measures the number of new users a typical existing user generates through referrals.

nodes.blurb: A viral growth loop is a self-reinforcing cycle in which every new user contributes to acquiring more users, creating compounding growth.

nodes.title: White-Labeling Model
nodes.blurb: White-labeling is a revenue model where a producer manufactures or develops a product/service that another company then rebrands and sells as its own.

nodes.blurb: Willingness to pay (WTP) is the maximum price a customer will accept for a product or service before walking away.
